reviews.review_description: However this one seems a little hard right from the start. Maybe its me but I feel the odds are against you the entire time either way still fun.

The game is distilled down to gun-fighting with your deputies against men in black.  I like that.    
The six shooter mechanics are fun and the audio effects for the guns are nice.  I have two S&W revolvers IRL, so this game resonates.   I have "plateaued" and cannot beat level 5, which might(?) be the last level.  The men-in-black just keep coming and once my deputies get shot up again, all the shots are aimed at me with no effective cover.  I agree with another review that it would be great to be able to move a bit more around the street and buildings.  The experience would/could be more like scenes from movies like "Pale Rider".    Alternately, maybe there could be ~3 locations to start from in level 5, with the first being easier.  Or maybe the variation could be in the number of men-in-black. (e.g. higher vantage point).  I think the guys up on the roof, behind the hay bales in level 5, are too hard to hit. Maybe some invisible "hit/physics" geometry is too "big".  
Adding hints on how to get the best accuracy would be welcome.  
I hope development continues on this fun Western shooter!

reviews.review_title: For Less Than a Fistful of Dollars
reviews.review_description: I don’t know what all to say. This game is impossible. Some of the aiming physics seems a touch off. But for $1, this is some of the most fun I’ve had on the Quest. I can’t beat Level 3, but cussing my way through every try is awesome. It would be cool to have a huge open space to run/evade in.

Good gun physics but the reloading system is very clunky and unreliable. The game is also downright impossible due to the amount of enemies from all angles firing at once. I can’t make it past level 3 after half an hour, leading to boring gameplay. It also has very limited movement with no smooth turn, a very small radius you can walk and glitchy mechanics. Overall I would rate a 2.5 star but a 2 will do
